Constructions.Window_Sgl_Clr_3mm:
!===============================================================================
! WindowConstructs.idf
!
! EnergyPlus Reference Data Set for Window Constructions
! Terminology used in construction names:
!
! Sgl            Single pane
! Dbl            Double pane
! Trp            Triple pane
! Quadruple      Quadruple pane
! Xmm            X = thickness of glass panes in mm
! Xmm/Ymm        X = thickness of glass panes, Y = gap thickness
! Clr            All panes are clear glass
! Bronze, Grey, Green, Blue  One pane is tinted the indicated color
! Tint           One pane is tinted
! Low Iron       Glass pane with low iron content
! Pyr            Pyrolytic coating
! Ref-X-Y        One pane has a metallic reflective coating where:
!                  X=A: stainless steel coating
!                    B: titanium coating
!                    C: pewter coating
!                    D: tin-oxide coating
!                  Y=L: low-transmittance coating
!                    M: medium-transmittance coating
!                    H: high-transmittance coating
!
! LoE            Low-emissivity metallic coating on one or more panes
! eX=Y           Low-emissivity coating on pane surface X with emissivity Y
!                   (surfaces are numbered from outside to inside)
! Spec Sel       Spectrally selective
! Film (XX)      Coated polyester film with nominal visible transmittance of XX percent
! Air            Between-pane gas fill is air
! Arg            Between-pane gas fill is argon
! Krypton        Between-pane gas fill is krypton
! Elec Abs       Electrochromic glass that darkens by becoming more absorptive
! Elec Ref       Electrochromic glass that darkens by becoming more reflective
! Bleached       Bleached (lightest) state of electrochromic glass
! Colored        Colored (darkest) state of electrochromic glass
!
! Immediately preceding each construction are the following - after the exclamation point (!):
!
! U              Nominal Center-of-glass U-value (W/m2-K) calculated
!                for winter conditions (-18C outside air temperature,
!                21C inside air temperature, 5.5 m/s windspeed and zero solar radiation).
! SC             Shading coefficient of the construction
! SHGC           Solar heat gain coefficient of the construction calculated
!                for summer conditions (32C outside air temperature,
!                24C inside air temperature, 2.8 m/s windspeed and
!                783 W/m2 incident beam solar radiation normal to glazing
! TSOL           Solar transmittance at normal incidence of the construction
! TVIS           Visible transmittance at normal incidence of the construction
! U=5.894  SHGC=0.861  TSOL=0.837  TVIS=0.898
! The objects and syntax in this dataset comply with EnergyPlus, Version 8.4.0 and above, unless otherwise noted.

  Construction,
    Sgl Clr 3mm,             !- Name
    CLEAR 3MM;

Windows.Glass_Clear_3mm:
!- Conductivity {W/m-K}

  WindowMaterial:Glazing,
    LoE SPEC SEL CLEAR 3MM,  !- Name
    SpectralAverage,         !- Optical Data Type
    ,                        !- Window Glass Spectral Data Set Name
    .003,                    !- Thickness {m}
    .450,                    !- Solar Transmittance at Normal Incidence
    .340,                    !- Front Side Solar Reflectance at Normal Incidence
    .370,                    !- Back Side Solar Reflectance at Normal Incidence
    .780,                    !- Visible Transmittance at Normal Incidence
    .070,                    !- Front Side Visible Reflectance at Normal Incidence
    .060,                    !- Back Side Visible Reflectance at Normal Incidence
    .0,                      !- Infrared Transmittance at Normal Incidence
    .84,                     !- Front Side Infrared Hemispherical Emissivity
    .03,                     !- Back Side Infrared Hemispherical Emissivity
    .9;
